composition.Programmer 1:
Implement backend code in app.py
- Develop the main backend Python application using Flask.
- The website should be accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/' (Dashboard).
- Implement routing for all nine pages plus the write review page, ensuring each route corresponds to frontend templates.
- Implement file read/write operations on all data files in the 'data' directory using plain text starting from the first line, without encryption.
- Implement business logic for browsing restaurants, viewing menus, managing cart, checkout, order tracking, and reviews.
- Frontend templates to be supported  dashboard.html, restaurants.html, menu.html, item_details.html, cart.html, checkout.html, active_orders.html, track_order.html, reviews.html, write_review.html.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop frontend templates and static assets
- Complete all HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory corresponding to the nine pages plus write review page.
- Ensure all URLs in frontend code use Flask routes, not direct HTML file names.
- Implement UI elements as specified in the requirements document for each page, including IDs and page titles.
- Develop CSS stylesheets in 'static/css/styles.css' for consistent styling.
- Develop JavaScript in 'static/js/scripts.js' for client-side interactivity such as button actions, filtering, and navigation.
- Integrate images (restaurant logos, menu item photos) in 'static/images/' referenced in the UI.
